In such event, the. site might be

At the same time, engineers are
at work making surveys and esti-
mates, and experts are at work
making elaborate calculations and
comparisons ito determine the re-
lation of cost to profit anticipated,
and all such questions. These pre-
liminaries take time, and Professor
Newell says now he will hardly be
able to get to the stage of making
a recommendation before spring. 1t
was hoped for a time that the an
nuai report Of the secretary Of the
interior would contain something
definite as to the beginning of the
work.


Considerable hard feeling has
irrigation experts of the different
government departments by the ac-
ton Of the secretary of the interior
in turning over the whole work to
the geological survey. The depart
ment of agriculture has a corps of
irrigationists who make investiga-
ticns and turn out literature on the
subject from time to tine, and they
think they should be consulted. I'm
